Puoi discutere anche con Putin ma n quel negoziato deve esserci 07:09 Il presidente ucraino Volodymyr Zelensky ha celebrato "tre anni di resistenza" nel terzo anniversario dell'invasione russa dell'Ucraina.  In un post sui social media, Zelensky ha parlato di "tre anni di resistenza. Tre anni di gratitudine. Tre anni di assoluto eroismo degli ucraini", ha scritto. "Ringrazio tutti coloro che difendono e sostengono l'Ucraina".  07:03 La Turchia "può contribuire ai colloqui di pace (tra Russia e Ucraina), anche come (Paese) ospitante". Lo ha detto il presidente turco, Recep Tayyip Erdogan, durante un colloquio telefonico con l'omologo francese, Emmanuel Macron, affermando l'importanza di "lanciare un processo negoziale che non escluda alcuna delle parti nel conflitto". Durante la telefonata, il leader turco ha sottolineato come Ankara continui "con i suoi sforzi per porre fine alla guerra tra Russia e Ucraina e stabilire una pace equa e duratura" e come la Turchia sia stato l'unico Paese "che ha portato le parti in guerra attorno al tavolo del negoziato in varie occasioni", riferisce la presidenza della Repubblica turca. 06:37 I leader dell'Ue sono arrivati ;;stamattina a Kiev per esprimere il loro sostegno all'Ucraina, nel terzo anniversario dell'invasione russa. "Siamo a Kiev oggi perché l'Ucraina è Europa. In questa lotta per la sopravvivenza non è solo il destino dell'Ucraina ad essere in gioco, ma quello dell'Europa", ha affermato la presidente della Commissione europea Ursula von der Leyen in un messaggio sui social media accompagnato da un video del suo arrivo in treno a Kiev insieme al presidente del Consiglio europeo Antonio Costa. Da non perdere
en_title: Trump assures: 'The war could end in a few weeks'. Putin: 'Europe can participate in peace'
en_content: 22:54 The United States resolution was adopted with ten votes in favour, zero against and five abstentions, after all the amendments (both those of Moscow and those of Europeans with Britain) had been rejected for procedural reasons or for the veto of Russia. Between the abstained, France and Britain two permanent members who could have used their veto power. 22:53 Russia has vetoed on two of the EU amendments on Ukraine which had reached the number of yes (more than nine) necessary to be approved in the UN Security Council. A third EU amendment has not received the necessary consents. The Russian amendments have been rejected for the same reason. 22:01 The "chief of the Kiev regime" Volodymyr Zelenksy "has problems, since consent for him is half that for Zaluzhny". Russian President Vladimir Putin, quoted by the Tass, referred to the former head of the Ukrainian armed forces, General Valery Zaluzhny, destituted by Zelensky and possible candidate for the succession of the Ukrainian leader. 21:50 "Thanks to Donald Trump for his words. Italy, the United States and Europe share common values and responsibilities. We will work together to address global challenges with determination and vision." The premier Giorgia Meloni says it in a tweet. 21:49 "Italy has been in these three difficult years and there will be, together with the rest of Europe and the West, for a future of sovereignty, prosperity and above all freedom." It is one of the concepts expressed by premier Giorgia Meloni at the G7 video conference meeting on the occasion of the third anniversary of Russian aggression against Ukraine, as a note on the government website reports. 21:30 "No Italian soldier in Ukraine". The League specifies it, after the words of the President of France Emmanuel Macron on the sending of European troops. 21:25 Russian President Vladimir Putin said that Europeans "can participate" in the solution of the conflict in Ukraine. "Russia is ready to cooperate with the United States, also accepting American investments on the exploitation of rare lands in Donbass". Vladimir Putin said so, according to the Tass report. Russian and American companies are "in contact" for joint economic projects. 21:19 "I spoke with Zelensky and Putin, many good things are happening." Donald Trump said in a press conference at the White House with Emmanuel Macron. 21:01 Emmanuel Macron corrects Donald Trump. The French President kindly interrupted Trump when he spoke, in the Oval Studio, about how Europe granted loans to Ukraine and will get his money back. Macron took the word and specified: "We actually paid 60% of the total effort. As with the United States, the form of "loans, guarantees and subsidies. We have 230 billion dollars of frozen assets in Europe, Russian assets. But this is not the guarantee of a loan because it is not ours. They're frozen." "Peace in Ukraine cannot be the surrender of the country." Emmanuel Macron said that at the press conference with Donald Trump. "No one wants to live in a world where the law of the strongest and international boundaries can be violated from day to day," added the French president. 21:00 "The meeting with Macron was a step forward towards peace in Ukraine." Donald Trump said that in a press conference at the White House with the French president. "Usa and France fought together in different conflicts and together we will end the bloody war in Ukraine" said US President. Trump says today's meeting with Macron was a "important step forward" towards achieving peace. For the American president he and the head of the Elysée agree on "many of the most important issues", including the fact that the conflict could evolve "in a third world war". "We will not allow this to happen," he said. 19:39 Donald Trump, asked about possible customs duties with Italy, praised the leadership qualities of Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni. "It is a great leader," he said, underlining that Italy is a "very important ally" for the United States. 19:20 The war in Ukraine could end within a few weeks and Vladimir Putin will accept European peace troops in Ukraine. Donald Trump assured him, after his meeting at the White House with Emmanuel Macron. The French President, for his part, stressed that Ukraine must be involved in peace talks. 17:53 The U.S. resolution on war in Ukraine was adopted by the UN General Assembly with the amendments proposed by the EU. Italy voted in favour of the amended text. The House approved the amendments tabled by France on behalf of the EU to the US resolution on Ukraine 'Path to Peace'. The votes were 78 yes, 16 no and 67 abstained; in the second case 60 yes, 18 no and 81 abstained. The U.S. resolution with amendments was adopted with 93 votes in favour, 73 abstentions and eight votes against (Belarussia, Burkina Faso, North Korea, Mali, Nicaragua, Niger, Russia, Sudan). The United States is among the abstained in the vote of the resolution presented by themselves, after this has been amended by the UN Assembly with the European proposal reiterated the Ukrainian territorial integrity and the demand for a "right peace". The Russian amendment to the American resolution was rejected: they voted against 71 countries, 59 abstained and 31 stood in favour. The U.S. had asked to reject all the amendments. 18:57 Donald Trump will soon meet Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky. He said that, according to the agency Bloomberg, the American president underlines that Zelensky could "come this week or next to sign the agreement" on the minerals, on which "we are close to an agreement". As for the meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin there will be soon. 17:48 The General Assembly of the UN reiterated its support for the territorial integrity of Ukraine. The General Assembly adopted the Ukrainian resolution supported by the EU with 93 yes, 18 no and 65 abstained. We are now voting on amendments to the United States resolution. The United States voted against the Ukrainian resolution, supported by the EU, on the territorial integrity of Ukraine. 17:24 The United States appealed to the Member States to remove the amendments of Russia and the EU to their resolution, which do nothing but "perpetuate the war of words". US ambassador Dorothy Shea stated that the US resolution is not "the peace agreement" but "the path to peace". "We will veto a possible Russian amendment and veto the European ones," said a senior official of the State Department protected by anonymity. The official added that the Trump administration considers the Security Council's vote important, not what is about to occur in the General Assembly on the third anniversary of the beginning of the war in Ukraine. "The goal is to maintain the resolution to the bone of what President Trump wants us to do and that is to use the United Nations to find a path of peace," he explained again. According to diplomatic sources, the four EU members of the Security Council plus Britain intend to table amendments to the US text to describe Russia as the aggressor and insist on the territorial integrity of Ukraine. The EU has also tabled amendments on this line to the US resolution at the General Assembly, where no country has veto power. 17:23 U.S. and Ukrainian resolutions and amendments to the U.S. text are now voting for the UN General Assembly. Before the vote, Chinese ambassador Fu Cong said that all efforts in favour of peace should be supported. It was underlined that a binding peace agreement should be acceptable to all parties and expressed the hope that Europe will find unity to address the deep causes of the conflict. "At this critical moment, we are counting on the international community to create a favourable atmosphere for a political solution to the crisis and the General Assembly to help build a consensus for peace between the Member States," said the Chinese ambassador. 17:15 The United States wants their resolution on Ukraine in the Security Council to pass without amendments and are ready to veto to block them. A high U.S. official said that anonymously. The Security Council should meet in the afternoon now in New York, the evening in Italy. 17:14 The representatives of Russia and the EU have tabled amendments to the American resolution to the UN. The Russian text, in the operational paragraph, 'includes the fact of facing the causes at the root of the conflict' after the phrase 'a rapid end of the conflict'. The first European amendment calls for the General Assembly to replace the phrase 'lasting peace between Ukraine and the Russian Federation' with the phrase 'just, lasting and total peace between Ukraine and the Russian Federation in line with the UN Charter and the principles of sovereignty, equality and territorial integrity of the States'. The second EU amendment proposes the addition of a new paragraph: "Reaffirming its commitment to the sovereignty, independence, unity and territorial integrity of Ukraine within its internationally recognized borders, including territorial waters." 17:04 The United States asked Russia and Ukraine to support their peace plan at the UN three years from the beginning of the Moscow war against Kiev. American ambassador Dorothy Shea presented in the general assembly the text entitled Path to Peace, 'Sentiero per la pace'. Shea noted the many resolutions that in three years asked Russia to withdraw troops from Ukraine without being able to stop the war. The US envoy added that Member States must return to the principles and purpose of the UN Charter to maintain international peace and security through the settlement of disputes. "While we are gathered here on the third anniversary, we need a resolution that marks the commitment of all Member States to bring a lasting end to war," and the US resolution "serves precisely to this," he added. 17:01 The Deputy Minister of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine, Betsa Mariana, presented to the UN General Assembly the resolution of Kiev for the end of the war. "This is a historical moment. As we respond today to the Russian resolution it will define the future of Ukraine, Europe and our common future," he said to the Member States of the Assembly. adding that "no country can feel safe if aggression is justified." 16:58 Ukraine is "an example of merit, quality and speed" in the reform process and "if it continues at this rate" its entry into the EU "may be before 2030". This was said by the President of the European Commission, Ursula von der Leyen, in Kiev. "I am deeply impressed by the speed and quality with which Ukraine is implementing the reforms and taking all necessary steps" to enter the Union, said von der Leyen, praising the "hard work" carried out by Kiev "while fighting a war for survival" and its "political will". 16:08 The President of the European Council, Antonio Costa, reiterated the need for the EU to appoint a special envoy for Ukraine for future peace talks. The theme will be on the table of the extraordinary summit of the leaders of the Twenty-seven in Brussels, convened for March 6. The goal, he emphasized Costa in Kiev, is "strengthening Ukrainian defence ability but also" define "how can we be prepared for future peace negotiations, if and when there will be". 16:30 A new meeting will be held tomorrow at Riad between the delegations of Russia and the United States, after that of last week between the Moscow Foreign Minister Serghei Lavrov and the American Secretary of State Marco Rubio who had established a regular "mechanism of consultation". A diplomatic source refers to the Afp, requiring anonymity. According to the source, "the follow-up meeting between representatives of Russia and USA will be "lower" than the previous one, "but there remains a signal of progress". 16:07 "If Ukraine falls all countries similar to ours", or the former Soviet republics, "like Estonia, are at risk because of the policies of the Kremlin." This was said by Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky speaking at the press conference with EU leaders and a representative of European leaders. Zelensky recalled that Russia justified the invasion of Donbass or Crimea as a "defense of Russian territories". 15:59 Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky had a hard fight with American Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent during their meeting in Kiev in the past few weeks. According to the Financial Times, the two would have clashed on the details of the agreement on minerals between the United States and Ukraine, and in particular on the $500 billion figure estimated by Americans for military assistance. Some Ukrainian officials reported that Zelensky was very angry and heard the screams coming from the room they were meeting. Bessent asked Zelensky to sign the agreement immediately, which still remains the subject of negotiations with Kiev that has so far resisted American pressure to close an agreement that does not include security guarantees. 15:01 A few hours after the vote, it is still a clash on the UN resolution on the third anniversary of the Russian invasion in Ukraine. The United States, writes the Financial Times, asked that the resolution of Ukraine and EU be withdrawn and instead proposed its own text, which equals the actions of Ukraine to those of Russia and weeps "the tragic loss of human lives in the conflict between the Russian Federation and Ukraine". The co-signed text of Ukraine and Europe condemns the Russian invasion and the vote is scheduled for this afternoon among the 193 members of the General Assembly. According to a document viewed by the Financial Times, France, supported by European allies, proposed an amendment to the American resolution referring to the "invasion of Ukraine by Russia" and to the "subsidiarity, independence, unity and territorial integrity of Ukraine". If the amendment was not accepted, most European countries would not vote in favour of the resolution, they warned officials, while recognizing that some might differ from the EU's position to support that of the United States. Two officials added that European members will repeat the amendment when the Security Council will meet later in the afternoon, but they said that its inclusion could meet the veto of Russia and China. France and the United Kingdom, which are both permanent members of the Council of 15 members, may therefore have to decide whether to use their vetoes to block the U.S. resolution or abstain and let it vote. 14:39 "We are concerned about the attempt to decide on the heads of Europeans and Ukrainians to make an agreement with Russia. This is unacceptable to Europe." "I'm rather concerned about what I hear in Washington, but I don't think it's the last word," added Merz, answering a question about the positions assumed in the US, both on duties and on Ukraine. 14:03 The United Kingdom launched "the largest package of sanctions" against Moscow from the beginning of the war in Ukraine on the day on which the third anniversary takes place. It is what we read in a note from the Foreign Office that 107 entities and individuals have been targeted both in Russia and in other countries that contribute to the military effort of the Kremlin and the economic one. Among them the North Korean Defence Minister No Kwang Chol as well as generals and senior officials of Pyongyang "complete with the deployment of over 11,000 troops" of North Korea in Russia. London's wide-range sanctions affect manufacturers and suppliers of equipment and components for the Russian army, including microprocessors within weapon systems, in different countries, such as Turkey, Thailand, India and China. For the first time, we always read in the note of the Foreign Ministry, new powers are used against foreign financial institutions that support "the war machine" in Moscow, such as the Ojsc Keremet Bank based in Kyrgyzstan. Among the Russian entities, a company accused of European technology transfer, as well as 14 oligarchs, such as Roman Trotsenko was sanctioned. In addition, 40 other ships of the so-called "shade fire" used by Moscow were targeted to continue the trade in oil and gas in the world by circumventing the embargo imposed by the West. "Every broken military supply line, every ruble blocked and every accomplice of Putin's unmasked aggression is a step towards a just and lasting peace," said Foreign Minister David Lammy. And again: "A lasting peace can only be achieved through force. That is why we focus on putting Ukraine into the strongest possible position." 13:57 "No one in Europe wants the war to continue, so we want to stop the Russian aggressor." The High Representative, Kaja Kallas, said this in a press conference at the edge of the Foreign Affairs Council. Kallas added that the 27 Foreign Ministers had a preliminary discussion on a military aid package. "The details and above all the amount will be discussed on the occasion of the extraordinary European summit, on March 6," explained emphasizing the need to "put Ukraine into a strong position" 13:56 "Why are all honest people beside Ukraine today? Why do free nations pay tribute to the heroes and victims of this inhuman war today? Because this is a war in defense of all of us: Ukraine, Poland, Europe and our values. That no one tries to question the very meaning of this struggle." Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk writes it in a tweet. 13:54 "Three years have passed since the beginning of the war launched by Russia to invade Ukraine" and "all this time, the Italian government has been at your side in the effort for a just peace. This cannot be the surrender of Ukraine. Italy is working with its friends and partners for peace. Peace is our goal! There will be no negotiations without Ukraine and Europe, it is impossible to reach a peace agreement without Ukraine and Europe, this is very important for us." Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani said this in his speech at the International Summit on the Support of Ukraine on the third anniversary of the invasion. "Peace must arrive with strong security guarantees for Ukraine. It must be real, strong and lasting, to prevent Europe from facing war again," added the vice-premier. "Italy is at the forefront of reconstruction, with particular attention to the energy sector, crucial for families, especially in winter. We are working hard, together with you and our friends and partners, to make the International Conference on Reconstruction that we will host in Rome on July 10 and 11 a great success," the minister explained. "For three difficult years, Italy has been alongside Ukraine, and will continue to do so, for a future of peace, growth, prosperity and, above all, freedom. Slava Ukraini!" he concluded. 13:53 "Let us continue to be at the side of the Ukrainians, for a just and lasting peace," wrote Chancellor Olaf Scholz in a message on social media X published in German, English and Ukrainian. Scholz also reiterated: "For three years, the Ukrainians defended their country with courage and heroism against the war of Russian aggression. Day after day. Week after week. No one wants peace more than them." 12:47 American President Donald Trump has "changed global debate" in recent weeks and has "created an opportunity" for peace in Ukraine but serve security guarantees in Kiev. This was said by British Prime Minister Keir Starmer in video connection with European and international leaders gathered in Kiev for the third anniversary from the beginning of the conflict with Russia. "If we want peace to last, Ukraine must have a seat at the negotiating table - Starmer has emphasized - and an agreement must be based on a sovereign Ukraine, supported by strong security guarantees". "The United Kingdom is ready and willing to support all this with troops on the ground together with other Europeans and with the right conditions in place," said the Prime Minister, reaffirming London's commitment to contribute military forces to a peace contingent once reached an agreement with Moscow on the end of the war. In this context, however, there must be the "vital" American support "to dissuade Russia from launching another invasion within a few years". Starmer also stated that Moscow "does not have all the cards" in hand in negotiations, contradicting what Trump said earlier. Then to say that the countries gathered in the G7 "would be ready to take more risks" by targeting the oil giants and the banks of Russia "that allow to evade Western sanctions". Sir Keir's intervention opened by paying tribute to President Volodymyr Zelensky's leadership and recalling that "for three years we were united" against the invasion of Moscow troops. The Prime Minister is preparing for the crucial face-to-face to the White House with Trump on Thursday with the peace talks in the centre to end the conflict where he will try to carry out the initiative, according to French President Emmanuel Macron, on the deployment of 30,000 European peacekeepers, as emerged on the media, possible though only with the US defensive shield. 12:20 The teams of Ukraine and the United States of America are in the final stages of the negotiations on rare land and the negotiations "are very constructive". It was written on X the first ministra for the European and Euro-Atlantic integration of Ukraine, Olga Stefanishyna, as Rbc Ukraina reports. "We are committed to completing it quickly to proceed with the signature. We hope that the leaders of the United States and Ukraine will sign it and approve it in Washington as soon as possible to demonstrate our commitment for decades to come," wrote Stefanishyna. 12:00 Moscow reached an agreement with Kiev for the return of an imprecisate number of inhabitants of the Russian Kursk region that are currently located on the Ukrainian territory in the Sumy region. "There are people who are today in Sumy and there is an agreement with the Red Cross and with the Ukrainian part for which they will be evacuated in Russia through Belarus," said Tatiana Moskalkova, Commissioner for Human Rights of Russia. 11:56 "There will be no credible and successful negotiations, nor lasting peace, without Ukraine and the EU. Only Ukraine can decide when there will be conditions to start peace talks. Peace cannot consist of a simple ceasefire, it must be a lasting agreement. Peace must not reward the aggressor." This was said by the President of the European Council, Antonio Costa, in Kiev. "Putin wants to divide us and today we demonstrate that our support is strong, united and immuted," he stressed, reaffirming the need for "concrete and strong security guarantees will guarantee a just and lasting peace in Ukraine and throughout Europe". 11:33 China welcomes "the positive efforts made by Russia and other stakeholders to resolve the crisis" in Ukraine. President Xi Jinping, speaking on the phone with his Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin at the request of the latter, recalled that last September China, Brazil and some countries of the South global " jointly founded the group 'Friends of Peace' on the Ukrainian crisis to create an atmosphere and accumulate conditions to promote a political solution to the crisis". Putin informed Xi "on the latest developments in contacts between Russia and the United States", Cctv reported. 10:56 Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky asked for a "real and lasting peace" in 2025 during the summit with some Western leaders, including the leadership of EU institutions, organized in Kiev on the third anniversary of the Russian invasion of Ukraine. The Ukrainian leader proposed a complete exchange of all prisoners of war with Russia as the "start" of the process to end the conflict. "Russia must release the Ukrainians. Ukraine is ready to trade everything for everyone, and this is a right way to start," he said. 10:31 Air alert in Kiev during the visit of some Western leaders, including the leaders of the European institutions,; in the capital to reaffirm their support on the third anniversary of the Russian invasion. The Ukrainian air force said it launched the alert at national level because of the threat of a Russian missile attack. 10:06 Russia does not want an immediate ceasefire along the current line of the front in Ukraine to discuss later the conditions of peace. Russian Foreign Minister Serghei Lavrov said so, quoted by the agency Ria Novosti. Moscow will cease hostilities when agreeable agreements will be reached for both parties, and when there will be an agreement that meets its demands, he added. "We are ready to negotiate with both Ukraine and Europe, with all the representatives who will want to contribute to peace. However, we will stop "Ukraine should not be part of NATO, this is indisputable", warned Lavrov, during a joint press conference with Fidan, Ankara, broadcast by Trt State TV. American president Donald "Trump clearly said that the entry of Ukraine into NATO would be a mistake", he added. The Russian Foreign Minister therefore reiterated that "we maintain our position, we have not changed our position" on Ukraine. "All parties must show realistic attitudes," he added, underlining that Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky "changes his position every time. This also applies to European countries, their views change. The U.S. has a consistent position. They want a ceasefire." 10:03 French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer agreed to convey a unitary message about Ukraine when they meet Donald Trump this week. High-level diplomatic sources said that the two men will try to convince Trump not to continue bilateral peace negotiations with Putin in exchange for the European commitment to the future security of Ukraine. The Times. Macron will meet US President today while Starmer will face the White House on Thursday. 09:45 "Europe is here to strengthen Ukraine at this critical moment. I can announce that a new payment of 3.5 billion euros for Ukraine will arrive in March. And as President Costa has just said, there will be a special European Council. I will present a complete plan on how to increase our weapons production and our defence capabilities in Europe. And Ukraine will also benefit from it. No one wants more peace than the Ukrainian people. But a just and lasting peace is achieved only by force." President Ursula von der Leyen said so in Kiev. Von der Leyen intervened at the International Summit in Kiev, which also raised the President of the European Council Antonio Costa, the Secretary-General of the OSCE Feridun Sinirlioglu and some European leaders, including the first Ministers or Heads of State of Spain, Finland, Lithuania, Latvia, Sweden, Norway, Iceland, Denmark, Estonia. Also present Justin Trudeau, Prime Minister of Canada. "The war in Ukraine remains the most central and most important crisis for the future of Europe. Putin is more than ever trying to win this war on the field. Its goal remains the capitulation of Ukraine. And then we know what might happen next. Because it has already happened in the past. The fate of Ukraine is not at stake. It is the fate of Europe. Our first priority remains to give strength to the resistance of Ukraine. So far, our Union and the Member States have supported Ukraine with EUR 134 billion. It's more than anyone else's," von der Leyen said. 09:22 The winner of the German election Friedrich Merz recalled, with a message on social media, the three years of Russian war in Ukraine, "three years of war in Europe". For Merz, Ukraine must be put in a strong position, that is why "for a just peace, the attacked country must participate in the peace negotiations". The conservative leader also recalled: "For three years we have been accompanied by terrible images of destruction and war crimes. Europe is still standing by the side of Ukraine." 09:21 "The EU keeps the promise: Foreign Ministers have just approved the 16th package of sanctions against Russia. It affects everything, from the shadow fleet ships to the game controllers used to control drones. Now we have the most extensive sanctions ever, which weaken Russia's war effort." The High Representative Ue Kaja Kallas writes on X. Within the sixteenth full package of sanctions adopted, the EU Council explains, Brussels has included in the blacklist 48 other persons and 35 entities responsible for actions that threaten territorial integrity, sovereignty and independence of Ukraine. Today's decision maintains pressure on the Russian army and defence, listing several industrial companies producing weapons, ammunition and other military equipment and technologies. As part of the efforts to close the networks of shadow fleets, the EU has also imposed sanctions to all effects against three entities carrying crude oil and Russian oil products and providing substantial revenue to the Russian government. In addition, this package continues to affect individuals responsible for evading EU sanctions, including through third countries. For the first time, the Council also decided to sanction a bag of cryptocurrencies based in Russia, Garantex, which is closely associated with Russian banks subject to EU sanctions. The sanctions will also be imposed on third-country actors who directly support Russian war effort. Today's lists include a Chinese company specializing in the production of satellite images, particularly for the benefit of the Russian military-industrial complex, along with its president, and two senior officers of the Korean People's Army. The response to Russian propaganda remains a priority. In addition to well-known Russian propagandists, the EU has included in the black list in particular NewsFront and SouthFront, two organs that present distorted interpretations of history and manipulated information in line with the Kremlin messaging, also addressing the European and world audience with distorted and manipulated information. 09:15 "Russia and its leadership are the only ones responsible for this war and atrocities committed against the Ukrainian people. We continue to ask that they respond for all war crimes and crimes against humanity committed. We welcome the recent steps taken towards the establishment of a Special Tribunal for the Crime of Aggression against Ukraine." This is read in a joint statement by the three European institutions, the Commission, Parliament and the Council on the third anniversary of Russian aggression against Ukraine. "The EU maintains its promise: Foreign Ministers have just approved the 16th package of sanctions against Russia. It affects everything, from the shadow fleet ships to the game controllers used to control drones. Now we have the most extensive sanctions ever, which weaken Russia's war effort." The High Representative Ue Kaja Kallas writes on X. 09:01 "It is in our fundamental interest that the negotiations on Ukraine "have success", for this reason "we will do everything possible to ensure that Brussels and Europeans in favor of war cannot block a peace agreement between the United States and Russia". Thus the Hungarian Foreign Minister, Péter Szijártó, via social. "We will not accept to accelerate with the extension of the regime of sanctions against individuals, we will not accept the outburst of tens of billion euros for the supply of weapons and as for security guarantees, we will not ask ourselves what security guarantees the Ukraine needs, but what security guarantees we need Hungarians" he added. 08:08 Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau arrived this morning in Kiev to commemorate the third anniversary of the Russian invasion. "I'm really happy to be here," said Trudeau coming down from the train, turning to Andriy Yermak, head of the Ukrainian presidential office. The images of the meeting were published on social media by Yermak himself. 07:51 "If I look at the messages that come from the United States, it is clear that Russian narrative is strongly represented." The High Representative Ue said that, Kaja Kallas entering the Foreign Affairs Council in Brussels, answering a question that US President Donald Trump is a victim of Russian disinformation. "I think it's good to have as many interactions with the new US administration as possible. Tomorrow I will also go to the United States to meet (the American Secretary of State) Marco Rubio and others to discuss these issues, because it is extremely important", Kallas explained, underlining the importance of "maintaining unity" transatlantic. "We must continue to support Ukraine and move forward together on this path. You can also discuss with Putin but that negotiation must be 07:09 Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky celebrated "three years of resistance" on the third anniversary of the Russian invasion of Ukraine. In a social media post, Zelensky spoke of "three years of resistance. Three years of gratitude. Three years of absolute heroism of the Ukrainians," he wrote. "I thank all those who defend and support Ukraine." 07:03 Turkey "can contribute to peace talks (between Russia and Ukraine), also as (country) host". The Turkish President, Recep Tayyip Erdogan, said this during a telephone interview with the French counterpart, Emmanuel Macron, stating the importance of "launching a negotiating process that does not exclude any of the parties in the conflict". During the call, the Turkish leader underlined how Ankara continued "with its efforts to end the war between Russia and Ukraine and establish a fair and lasting peace" and how Turkey was the only country "who brought the parties to war around the negotiating table on various occasions", reports the presidency of the Turkish Republic. 06:37 The leaders of the EU arrived; in Kiev to express their support for Ukraine, on the third anniversary of the Russian invasion. "We are in Kiev today because Ukraine is Europe. In this fight for survival it is not only the fate of Ukraine to be at stake, but that of Europe", said the President of the European Commission Ursula von der Leyen in a message on social media accompanied by a video of his arrival by train in Kiev together with the President of the European Council Antonio Costa. Not to be missed
en_subtitle: The UN Council adopts the US resolution. I'll see Zelensky this week or next. For tycoon Meloni 'is a great leader'

title: Putin: 'Intese economiche con gli Usa, Zelensky tossico'
subtitle: Lo zar indica le terre rare del Donbass. Telefonata con Xi. 'L'Europa? può partecipare alla pace' dice il presidente russo
content: , a partire dallo sfruttamento delle terre rare nel Donbass occupato, mentre le aziende dei due Paesi sarebbero già "in contatto" per progetti congiunti. A dispetto dei tentativi di Trump di incrinare questo asse. Il colloquio con Xi è avvenuto su iniziativa di Putin, proprio con lo scopo dichiarato di informare Xi sull'andamento dei contatti avviati con gli Usa, che hanno visto finora il momento più alto dal punto di vista simbolico con la telefonata fra Trump e Putin del 12 febbraio, e poi sono proseguiti la settimana scorsa a Riad con un incontro in presenza fra le delegazioni russa e americana guidate dai rispettivi ministri degli Esteri. Iniziative nelle quali diversi osservatori hanno visto un tentativo americano di allontanare Mosca dall'abbraccio di Pechino in preparazione di un inasprimento della sfida tra gli Stati Uniti e la Cina. Una manovra strategica in senso contrario rispetto a quella realizzata all'inizio degli anni '70 dal presidente Richard Nixon e dal suo segretario di Stato Henry Kissinger verso la Cina di Mao per mettere in difficoltà l'Unione Sovietica. Ogni sospetto sarebbe stato fugato, assicurano Pechino e Mosca, durante quella che il Cremlino ha definito una conversazione "lunga, calorosa e amichevole". La parte cinese, afferma l'ufficio di presidenza russo, ha anzi "espresso il suo sostegno al dialogo avviato tra Russia e Stati Uniti, nonché la sua disponibilità a contribuire alla ricerca di soluzioni pacifiche per risolvere il conflitto ucraino". La Cina, hanno confermato da Pechino, accoglie con favore "gli sforzi positivi compiuti dalla Russia e dalle altre parti interessate per risolvere la crisi" in Ucraina, ricordando che anche la Repubblica popolare ha fatto del suo, cercando di creare un'atmosfera favorevole al dialogo insieme con il Brasile e altri Paesi del cosiddetto Sud globale. Despite Trump's attempts to crack this axis. The interview with Xi took place on the initiative of Putin, precisely with the stated purpose of informing Xi on the progress of the contacts started with the U.S., which so far saw the highest moment from the symbolic point of view with the call between Trump and Putin of 12 February, and then continued last week at Riad with a meeting in presence between the Russian and American delegations led by the respective foreign ministers. Initiatives in which several observers saw an American attempt to remove Moscow from Beijing's embrace in preparation for a tightening of the challenge between the United States and China. A strategic manoeuvre contrary to that achieved in the early 1970s by President Richard Nixon and his Secretary of State Henry Kissinger towards Mao China to put the Soviet Union in trouble. Every suspect would have been escaped, assure Beijing and Moscow, during what the Kremlin called a "long, warm and friendly" conversation. The Chinese side, says the Russian Bureau, "expressed its support for the dialogue initiated between Russia and the United States, as well as its willingness to contribute to the search for peaceful solutions to resolve the Ukrainian conflict". China, confirmed by Beijing, welcomes "the positive efforts made by Russia and other stakeholders to resolve the crisis" in Ukraine, recalling that the People's Republic has also made its own, trying to create an atmosphere favourable to dialogue with Brazil and other countries of the so-called global South. Se la visita specialistica tramite il sistema sanitario richiede mesi d’attesa, il cittadino abbiente la fa privatamente. Io spero di poter risolvere il 50% dei problemi”, ha aggiunto il dottor Crotta. Facendo propri i numeri della povertà in Italia (il 7,6% secondo il Cnel nel 2024) in Valle d’Aosta, a detta dell’associazione Das, il bacino di utenza sarebbe di 9.300 cittadini. title: Dai coltelli ai gioielli, l'arte dei fratelli Giagu
subtitle: La Pattadesa si trasforma con le nuove generazioni di artigiani
content: A Pattada si diceva sempre che il coltello fosse il prolungamento della mano del pastore. Nessun allevatore era mai sprovvisto della sua "resolza", come si chiama in sardo il coltello con la lama richiudibile a forma di mirto e il manico in corno. E questo detto antico, arrivato fino ai giorni nostri, ha reso il coltello realizzato a Pattada, piccola cittadina di poco meno di tremila abitanti nel territorio del Monteacuto, tra Logudoro e Gallura, famoso in tutto il mondo. Se si pensa infatti ad un coltello identificativo della Sardegna, molto probabilmente si penserà a quel modello di coltello Pattadese, di Pattada appunto. Qui dal 1985, quando Salvatore Giagu e sua moglie Maria Rosaria Deroma aprirono la loro bottega di coltellai, la tradizione del coltello col manico di corno continua ad essere portata avanti e tramandata anche dai loro figli, Adriano e Letizia, seconda generazione di artigiani custodi di una raffinata competenza tecnica, ma portatori di idee innovative come solo le nuove generazioni sanno fare. Se Maria Rosaria Deroma è stata la prima donna in Italia a specializzarsi nella lavorazione artigianale del coltello, sua figlia Letizia, classe 1998, è la seconda. Nel laboratorio nel cuore del centro storico di Pattada, Letizia e suo fratello Adriano lavorano sapientemente alla realizzazione di questi pezzi unici, esclusivi perché acquistabili solo su prenotazione, indistruttibili e intrisi della tradizione sarda. "Da noi nessuno può regalare un coltello, lo si deve per forza acquistare. È una questione di scaramanzia, di credenza antica, perché la lama è un qualcosa che ferisce e quindi chi riceve in dono un coltello deve ripagarlo, fosse anche solo con una moneta da pochi euro", racconta all'ANSA Adriano, titolare della Giagu Coltelli, azienda che quest'anno festeggia 40 anni di attività. Trent'anni, una laurea in Ingegneria al Politecnico di Torino e un presente alla guida dell'azienda di famiglia, a cui Adriano ha deciso di dare una svolta nella produzione. "Oltre a costruire i coltelli della tradizione, abbiamo brevettato sette nuovi modelli e ci siamo affacciati al mercato dei coltelli da cucina. Nel rispetto dei metodi di lavorazione classici - spiega l'artigiano -, abbiamo creato una linea di coltelli pensati appositamente per chef, ristoranti e appassionati: dai classici multiuso per arrosti, salumi e formaggi, ai vari Trinciante, Santoku, coltelli da ostriche e coltelli da bracerie. Tra questi spicca la Leppa, coltello a lama larga ispirato alle antiche Leppas sarde, riproposto in chiave moderna". Se Adriano e sua sorella Letizia hanno guardato al mondo stellato della ristorazione, tanto da entrare negli hotel della Costa Smerlada come il Romazzino, A Belmond Hotel, uno dei più prestigiosi resort di Porto Cervo che ha scelto questi coltelli per la propria boutique, l'altra sorella Aurelia ha deciso di ingentilire la produzione di famiglia e, partendo dagli scarti di lavorazione del corno, ha fatto volare la sua immaginazione e le sue mani esperte di abile artigiana, anche lei all'interno del laboratorio di coltello di famiglia, creando una linea di gioielli. Collane, bracciali ed anelli: tutti pezzi unici, che uniscono la tradizione sarda a linee più moderne. "La mia idea è nata mentre lavoravo il coltello con il materiale del corno in bottega - svela Aurelia, fondatrice del marchio Giagu Design -. Nei ritagli di tempo mi sono resa conto che si trattava di un materiale leggero e che si prestava ad essere lavorato in diverse forme. Potevo creare qualcosa di particolare e personalizzabile. Il corno si presenta con innumerevoli striature ed è unico perché non si può mai replicare, uno dei miei gioielli non è mai uguale ad un altro. Ho pensato di combinarlo con altri materiali più preziosi come oro, argento, acciaio, corallo mediterraneo e infarcirlo di richiami alla tradizione della mia Isola, perché è da lì che parte tutto, rivisitando il gioiello sardo. Tutti i pezzi sono su ordinazione e hanno un costo elevato proprio per la loro unicità". Dai coltelli ai gioielli: la tradizione di Pattada guarda al futuro grazie alle nuove generazioni di artigiani come Aurelia, Letizia e Adriano Giagu, senza mai dimenticare le origini tramandate da mamma Maria Rosaria e babbo Salvatore. Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA
en_title: From knives to jewels, the art of the Giagu brothers
en_content: In Pattada it was always said that the knife was the extension of the shepherd's hand. No breeder was ever without his "resolza", as it is called in Sardinian the knife with the foldable blade in the form of myrtle and the horn handle. And this ancient saying, arrived until today, made the knife made in Pattada, a small town of just under three thousand inhabitants in the territory of Monteacuto, between Logudoro and Gallura, famous all over the world. If you think of an identification knife of Sardinia, you will most likely think of that model of Pattadese knife, by Pattada. Here since 1985, when Salvatore Giagu and his wife Maria Rosaria Deroma opened their workshop of knifemakers, the tradition of the knife with the horn handle continues to be carried forward and handed down also by their children, Adriano and Letizia, second generation of artisans guards of a refined technical competence, but bearers of innovative ideas as only the new generations can do. If Maria Rosaria Deroma was the first woman in Italy to specialize in the crafting of the knife, her daughter Letizia, class 1998, is the second. In the laboratory in the heart of the historic center of Pattada, Letizia and his brother Adriano work wisely to the realization of these unique pieces, exclusive because they can only be purchased by reservation, indestructible and intrigue of the Sardinian tradition. "No one can give a knife to us, you have to buy it. It is a question of scaramanzia, of ancient belief, because the blade is something that hurts and therefore who receives as a gift a knife must repay it, it is also only with a coin from a few euros", says Adriano, owner of Giagu Coltelli, company that this year celebrates 40 years of activity. Thirty years, a degree in Engineering at the Politecnico di Torino and a present at the head of the family company, to which Adriano decided to give a turn in production. "In addition to building traditional knives, we have patented seven new models and we have overlooked the kitchen knives market. In compliance with the classic processing methods - explains the artisan -, we have created a line of knives designed specifically for chefs, restaurants and enthusiasts: from the classic multipurpose for roasts, meats and cheeses, to the various Trinciante, Santoku, oyster knives and bracerie knives. Among these stands the Leppa, a wide blade knife inspired by the ancient Sardinian Leppas, reproduced in a modern key". If Adriano and his sister Letizia have looked at the starry world of catering, so much to enter the hotels of Costa Smerlada as the Romazzino, A Belmond Hotel, one of the most prestigious resorts of Porto Cervo who chose these knives for his own boutique, the other sister Aurelia has decided to engrave the production of family and, starting from the wastes of working of the horn, has made fly her imagination and her Necklaces, bracelets and rings: all unique pieces, which combine Sardinian tradition with more modern lines. "My idea was born while working the knife with the horn material in the shop - reveals Aurelia, founder of the brand Giagu Design -. In the time cutouts I realized that it was a light material and that it was suitable to be worked in different forms. I could create something special and customizable. The horn comes with countless striations and is unique because you can never replicate, one of my jewels is never equal to another. I thought of combining it with other precious materials such as gold, silver, steel, Mediterranean coral and making us call back to the tradition of my island, because it is from there that everything starts, revisiting the Sardinian jewel. All pieces are custom-made and have a high cost for their uniqueness." From knives to jewels: the tradition of Pattada looks to the future thanks to the new generations of artisans such as Aurelia, Letizia and Adriano Giagu, never forget the origins handed down by mother Maria Rosaria and babbo Salvatore. en_subtitle: The Pattadesa is transformed with the new generations of artisans

title: Canto per la pace in Palestina, sogno Eurovision
subtitle: Milad allo Zecchino d'Oro conobbe la famiglia italiana che lo aiutò
content: "Nessun muro può trattenere il sogno della libertà, nessuna catena può rubare il nostro destino". Sono alcune delle parole della canzone 'Humanity calls', l'umanità chiama, che il 30enne Fatouleh Milad, palestinese che vive stabilmente in Italia da quando aveva poco più di 11 anni, porterà alle semifinali del concorso che gli permetterebbe di rappresentare San Marino all'Eurovision. "In stazione - ricorda - venne tutta la famiglia a prenderci e ci portarono a Cassano dove avevano affittato per noi una casa con tutto il necessario per farci vivere dignitosamente. Era novembre. E il 27 dicembre, giorno del compleanno di mia madre, ci regalarono anche un'auto. E' una storia che sembra un film". Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA
en_title: Song for peace in Palestine, dream Eurovision
en_content: "No wall can hold the dream of freedom, no chain can steal our destiny." These are some of the words of the song 'Humanity calls', humanity calls, that the 30-year-old Fatouleh Milad, a Palestinian who lives permanently in Italy since he was just over 11 years old, will lead to the semi-finals of the contest that would allow him to represent San Marino at the Eurovision. "In the station - remember - the whole family came to pick us up and took us to Cassano where they rented for us a house with everything necessary to make us live dignified. It was November. And on December 27, my mother's birthday day, they also gave us a car. It's a story that looks like a movie." Reproduction reserved © Copyright ANSA

title: Un album di quasi mille figurine racconta una piccola comunità
subtitle: A Maiolati Spontini (Ancona) tra sport, associazioni e pesca
content: Un album con più di mille figurine, per raccontare i volti della vivace realtà associativa e sportiva del Comune di Maiolati Spontini, in provincia di Ancona, paese natale del grande compositore Gaspare Spontini. Un progetto pensato per rafforzare la passione e il senso di appartenenza alle piccole comunità locali, adottato nella piccola cittadina marchigiana, e ideato dall'agenzia "Akinda Italia srl", che si occupa della creazione di prodotti editoriali, in collaborazione con 16 associazioni. L'album in due volumi esce in questi giorni in due edicole della zona, insieme ai pacchetti di figurine con le foto dei tesserati delle associazioni, sportive e non, che aderiscono al progetto. "E' un contenitore unico, dove associazioni anche distanti fra loro, e iscritti di varie età, stanno insieme, a dimostrazione di una comunità coesa e unita", spiega il sindaco Tiziano Consoli. "Un bellissimo prodotto, - osserva il consigliere delegato alle Politiche giovanili Tommaso Ciampichetti - che avrà anche il pregio di far riscoprire, in particolare ai più piccoli, la bellezza di condividere l'emozione di aprire un pacchetto e scambiare delle figurine, anche come alternativa a passatempi ormai solo tecnologici, tanto più se queste raccontano la nostra comunità. La realizzazione del progetto non abbia comportato oneri né a carico dell'Amministrazione comunale né delle associazioni, che anzi ne potranno trarre un vantaggio in termini di visibilità". Un "ringraziamento speciale va ovviamente alle associazioni, per la forte partecipazione che ha prodotto, come risultato, il fatto che quasi tutte le famiglie del nostro Comune, che conta poco meno di 6 mila abitanti, abbia un suo rappresentante ritratto nelle figurine". Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA
en_title: An album of almost a thousand figurines tells a small community
en_content: An album with more than a thousand figurines, to tell the faces of the lively associative and sporting reality of the Municipality of Maiolati Spontini, in the province of Ancona, birthplace of the great composer Gaspare Spontini. A project designed to strengthen the passion and sense of belonging to the small local communities, adopted in the small town of Marche, and conceived by the agency "Akinda Italia srl", which deals with the creation of editorial products, in collaboration with 16 associations. The two-volume album is released these days in two newsstands in the area, along with figurine packs with photos of club members, sports and not, that adhere to the project. "It is a unique container, where associations also distant from each other, and members of various ages, are together, demonstrating a cohesive and united community," explains Mayor Tiziano Consoli. "A beautiful product, - notes the managing director to the youth policies Tommaso Ciampichetti - which will also have the merit of rediscovering, in particular to the little ones, the beauty of sharing the emotion of opening a package and exchange of figurines, also as an alternative to technological pastimes, much more so if they tell our community. The implementation of the project did not involve burdens either on the municipal administration or associations, which could indeed benefit in terms of visibility." A "special thanks goes obviously to the associations, for the strong participation that has produced, as a result, the fact that almost all the families of our Municipality, which counts little less than 6 thousand inhabitants, has its representative portrayed in the figurines". title: Spegne 110 candeline Luisetta, la più anziana della Sardegna
subtitle: Festa di compleanno in una Rsa di Cagliari
content: Ha attraversato due pandemie e due guerre e si è distinta per spirito di solidarietà durante gli sfollamenti. Oggi Luisetta Mercalli, la decana della Sardegna e la 18/a in Italia ha spento le sue 110 candeline. Proprio nel giorno della 82/a ricorrenza dei bombardamenti su Cagliari. Questa mattina è stata festeggiata al centro su Planu a Cagliari attorniata da amici, parenti e istituzioni: Marco Benucci, presidente del consiglio comunale di Cagliari le ha consegnato la medaglia per il traguardo raggiunto e la pergamena firmata dal sindaco Massimo Zedda con gli auguri del Consiglio e della città di Cagliari. Non è voluto mancare il sindaco di Carloforte, Stefano Rombi, dove la decana è nata nel 1915: si è sfilato la fascia tricolore e l'ha fatta indossare all'ultracentenaria. "Luisetta - spiega - è una figlia illustre di Carloforte, sono felice di portare gli auguri di tutta la comunità che nutre per lei un affetto smisurato per come si è distinta con grande generosità e spirito altruistico". Era il 4 aprile 1943 quando un raid aereo causò a Carloforte 12 morti. "Mamma è scesa in paese e ha imposto e organizzato la ricomposizione dei corpi perché i familiari potessero dare un dignitoso saluto ai loro cari", ricorda Myriam, una delle figlie. Luisetta si commuove: "ho fatto solo il mio dovere". Lo scorso anno a 109 anni, ha realizzato uno dei suoi sogni: assistere alla rappresentazione di Madama Butterfly al Teatro Lirico di Cagliari. E tuttora non si perde una rappresentazione di lirica e balletto col gruppo della rsa di su Planu.  La colonna sonora che ha accompagnato questa mattina i festeggiamenti è stata con le note del capolavoro pucciniano e la celebre aria "Un bel dì vedremo". Uno dei momenti più toccanti della festa organizzata dalle figlie Angela e Myriam con le nipoti Alice, Elena e Francesca e gli altri, per la sua grande passione per la musica che accompagna da sempre la sua lunga vita. Luisetta arrivò a 12 anni e mezzo nell'Istituto delle Suore Carlo Felice per studiare e si diplomò prestissimo a 16 anni nella Scuola di Metodo per l'Educazione Materna, oggi Istituto Pedagogico. Luisetta ha dovuto fare i conti anche la tragedia delle pandemie, da un millennio all'altro: superò la terribile spagnola a 3 anni e ha superato indenne anche quella del Covid. Vera pioniera per quel periodo, iniziò a insegnare giovanissima a soli 17 anni, perché, come racconta sorridendo, "fu costretta" dal preside ad accettare una supplenza di 15 giorni, proprio a Carloforte, come insegnante alla Scuola di Avviamento Professionale, e da allora non si è fermata più fino a 62 anni, con 46 anni di servizio. "Non mi aspettavo di arrivare a questa età - ha detto emozionata - grazie per questo affetto". Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA
en_title: Spegne 110 candeline Luisetta, the oldest in Sardinia
en_content: He crossed two pandemics and two wars and distinguished himself by the spirit of solidarity during the displacement. This morning it was celebrated in the center on Planu in Cagliari surrounded by friends, relatives and institutions: Marco Benucci, president of the municipal council of Cagliari has given her the medal for the finish line and the parchment signed by the mayor Massimo Zedda with the wishes of the Council and the city of Cagliari. The mayor of Carloforte, Stefano Rombi, did not want to miss, where the decana was born in 1915: the tricolor band paraded and made it wear to the ultracentennial. "Luisetta - he explains - is an illustrious daughter of Carloforte, I am happy to bring the best wishes of the whole community that feeds for her an immeasured affection for how it is distinguished with great generosity and altruistic spirit". It was April 4, 1943 when an air raid caused Carloforte 12 dead. "Mamma came down in the country and imposed and organized the recomposition of the bodies so that the family could give a dignified greeting to their loved ones," recalls Myriam, one of the daughters. And still there is not a representation of opera and ballet with the group of the su Planu rsa. Luisetta arrived at 12 and a half years in the Institute of the Sisters Carlo Felice to study and graduated very early at 16 years in the School of Method for Maternal Education, today Pedagogical Institute. Luisetta also had to deal with the tragedy of pandemics, from one millennium to another: He surpassed the terrible Spanish at 3 years and overcame the Covid. True pioneer for that period, he began teaching very young at only 17 years old, because, as he says smiling, "was forced" by the principal to accept a 15-day substitute, just in Carloforte, as a teacher at the School of Professional Poisoning, and since then he has not stopped until 62 years, with 46 years of service. "I didn't expect to get to this age - he said excited - thank you for this affection." Reproduction reserved © Copyright ANSA
en_subtitle: Birthday party in a Rsa of Cagliari

title: Morgan e la comunità dove 'non esistono ragazzi cattivi'
subtitle: A 20 anni dal carcere a Kairos, la realtà di don Burgio
content:
(di Gioia Giudici)
   Per cambiare una vita che sembra
già segnata, a volte bastano uno sguardo, una parola, un gesto
affettuoso. E' quel che è successo a Morgan, 20 anni, occhi
trasparenti e pieni di gratitudine per la vita, che ha messo più
volte sulla sua strada don Claudio Burgio, prima come fondatore
della comunità Kairos e poi come cappellano del carcere minorile
Beccaria. E' stato lì, dopo averlo riconosciuto come uno dei
suoi ex ospiti, "che è successo qualcosa - racconta Morgan - che
non mi sarei mai aspettato, è arrivato a parlarmi, a dirmi 'ma
allora sei un pirla', ma con dolcezza, come me lo avrebbe detto
un padre. Quando è finito il Beccaria mi ha ripreso ed è stato
allora che ho sentito per la prima volta che c'era qualcuno che
non pensava che fossi un mostro". Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA
en_title: Morgan and the community where 'there are no bad guys'
en_content:
(of Gioia Giudici)
To change a life that seems
already marked, sometimes just a look, a word, a gesture
affectionate. That's what happened to Morgan, 20 years, eyes.
transparent and full of gratitude for life, which has put more
times on his way Don Claudio Burgio, first as founder
of the Kairos community and then as a chaplain of the juvenile prison
Beccaria. He was there after he recognized him as one of the
his former guests, "which happened something - says Morgan - that
I never expected, he came to talk to me, to tell me 'but
Then you're a pigeon, but with sweetness, as I would have said
a father. When the Beccaria ended, I got back and it was
then that I heard for the first time that there was someone
He didn't think I was a monster." Reproduction reserved © Copyright ANSA
en_subtitle: 20 years from prison in Kairos, the reality of Don Burgio

title: Nasce a Treviso l''atelier sostenibile' degli abiti da sposa usati
subtitle: L'iniziativa mutuata da un'idea delle suore di un convento in Umbria. L'abito si sceglie su un catalogo e si prenota con una offerta libera
content: Il matrimonio, oltre che un impegno per la vita è anche un impegno economico non da poco. Organizzare la cerimonia, e poi comprare i fiori, l'abito, offrire il pranzo per gli ospiti, sono tutti elementi della festa che hanno costi di tutto rispetto . Non tutte le spose possono concedersi non solo l'abito dei loro sogni ma comunque un abito nuovo. A Treviso le donne che stanno per sposarsi hanno una possibilità in più.   Le taglie della casa trevigiana, che si possono visionare e provare nella taverna di un'abitazione resa disponibile da una volontaria, sono comprese tra la 38 e la 52, e i modelli spaziano dalle linee classiche alle più contemporanee. Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA
en_title: The sustainable atelier of used wedding dresses was born in Treviso
en_content: Marriage, as well as a commitment to life is also an economic commitment not recently. Organize the ceremony, and then buy the flowers, the dress, offer lunch for the guests, are all elements of the party that have costs of all respect . Not all brides can afford not only the dress of their dreams but still a new dress. In Treviso women who are getting married have an extra chance. The sizes of the Treviso house, which can be seen and tried in the tavern of a house made available by a volunteer, are between 38 and 52, and the models range from classic to more contemporary lines. Reproduction reserved © Copyright ANSA
en_subtitle: The initiative borrowed from an idea of the nuns of a convent in Umbria. The dress you choose on a catalog and book with a free offer

title: Fertilizzante dalla posidonia spiaggiata, progetto in Sardegna
subtitle: Il terriccio organico in un impianto unico in Europa a Quartu
content:
Dal trattamento dei
rifiuti spiaggiati della Sardegna nasce il primo terriccio
organico realizzato con la posidonia oceanica raccolta sulle
coste dell'isola. Si chiama "Posidonia garden": la sabbia
recuperata, che diversamente sarebbe finita in discarica o nel
retro spiaggia, viene lavata, depurata e riportata sull'arenile
di provenienza, contribuendo in questo modo a contrastare il
fenomeno dell'erosione costiera e mantenendo le caratteristiche
dimensionali e cromatiche delle spiagge dell'isola. La posidonia
una volta lavata, separata dalla sabbia, dai rifiuti e privata
dei cloruri diventa invece una nuova risorsa ricca di nutrienti. Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA
en_title: Fertilizer from the beached posidonia, project in Sardinia
en_content:
From the treatment of
Sardinian beach waste was born the first soil
organic made with oceanic posidonia collected on
coast of the island. It's called "Posidonia garden": the sand
recovered, which otherwise would end in landfill or in
retro beach, is washed, purified and reported on the arenile
of origin, contributing in this way to counteract the
phenomenon of coastal erosion and maintaining the characteristics
dimensional and chromatic of the island's beaches. Posidonia
once washed, separated from sand, waste and private
of chlorides instead becomes a new resource rich in nutrients. Reproduction reserved © Copyright ANSA
en_subtitle: The organic soil in a single plant in Europe in Quartu

title: Ad Ancona lezioni di vela per persone con disturbi psichici
subtitle: Dalla Regione Marche Terra di Benessere supporto al progetto
content: Lezioni di vela dedicate a chi soffre di disturbi psicologici, contestuali ad un percorso di psicoterapia a bordo, una regata in cui i partecipanti si integreranno con vari equipaggi in gara ma anche una traversata da Ancona alla Croazia per un'esperienza vera e propria di navigazione. Con il progetto "Una vela per tutti", promosso da Ancona Yacht Club insieme ad Anpis (Associazione Nazionale Polisportiva per l'Integrazione Sociale), le Marche si dimostrano Terra di Benessere anche sul fronte del supporto alle persone con disagio psichico. Con la legge 23 del 7 dicembre 2023, la Regione si è dotata di uno strumento innovativo per realizzare in maniera coordinata una serie di interventi per favorire lo sviluppo armonico e sostenibile dell'essere umano, della natura, dell'ambiente, promuovendo stili di vita sani e consapevoli; per promuovere le Marche come Terra del benessere e della qualità della vita a 360 gradi. In questo solco la Regione, tramite l'assessorato allo Sviluppo economico e all'Agricoltura guidato da Andrea Maria Antonini, affianca e contribuisce a Una vela per tutti con un contributo da 10mila euro, nell'ambito di un bando più ampio da 50mila euro rivolto a varie iniziative, ma con modalità innovativa: approntata una piattaforma di crowdfunding tramite la quale, chi promuove i progetti, dovrà raccogliere 10mila euro sul web per ottenere lo stesso importo dalla Regione Marche. In questo modo ammonteranno a 20mila euro i fondi a disposizione di "Una Vela per tutti", progetto ormai attivo da un decennio, che è stato presentato oggi nella Sala Agricoltura della sede regionale di Palazzo Leopardi insieme alle istituzioni e ai privati che collaborano e lo supportano: dal Comune di Ancona, presenti il vice sindaco Giovanni Zinni e l'assessora ai Servizi Sociali Manuela Caucci, all'Azienda sanitaria territoriale di Ancona fino all'imprenditore di Frittelli Maritime Group e velista, Alberto Rossi, sostenitore dalla prima ora dell'iniziativa. Grazie al contributo di tanti sostenitori che assicurano il raggiungimento dei 10mila euro in crowdfunding, "Una vela per tutti" dà la possibilità a 24 persone di frequentare queste lezioni di vela-psicoterapia; l'età minima parte dai 16 anni, con priorità per giovani: il primo incontro è avvenuto martedì scorso e da martedì prossimo inizieranno le lezioni tutte le settimane: prima teoriche poi in mare e con psicoterapia. Il percorso durerà fino a settembre quando i partecipanti, integrati in altri equipaggi, disputeranno la tradizionale Regata del Conero. In virtù del sostegno della Regione, rispetto allo scorso anno, raddoppierà il numero dei partecipanti che avrà la possibilità della traversata in Croazia: evento che si terrà in due settimane, tra giugno e luglio, ciascuna con un equipaggio di otto persone. A bordo c'è chi sta al timone, chi al fiocco, chi alla randa e chi magari fa foto o mette la musica che Quali sono questi ruoli? C'è chi sta al timone, c'è chi sta al fiocco, chi sta alla randa ma anche chi ha il ruolo di fotografo e chi ha il ruolo di mettere la musica e di socializzare. "Ognuno ha le sue caratteristiche - aggiunge - perché, per l'equipaggio ognuno tira fuori anche la sua personalità e ognuno è alla pari, mette le sue risorse". "Arrivano giovani che hanno perso la motivazione di vivere, - ricorda Grelloni -, che hanno perso la spinta vitale, si sentono un po' persi, senza motivo di esistere. Qui si sperimentano e si sentono utili per qualcuno perché in un equipaggio, siamo tutti utili e scoprono che forse non è tutto lì quello che si può vivere, c'è ancora speranza, c'è ancora possibilità di poter essere di aiuto a se stessi ma anche agli altri, di relazionarsi, di vedere la luce, di trovare la speranza su persone".
en_title: Sailing lessons for people with mental disorders in Ancona
en_content: Sailing lessons dedicated to those suffering from psychological disorders, contextual to a path of psychotherapy on board, a race where participants will integrate with various crews in the race but also a crossing from Ancona to Croatia for a real experience of sailing. With the project "A sail for everyone", promoted by Ancona Yacht Club together with Anpis (National Association for Social Integration), the Marches prove themselves to be Land of Wellness also on the front of support for people with mental discomfort. With Law 23 of 7 December 2023, the Region has provided an innovative tool to co-ordinately implement a series of interventions to promote the harmonious and sustainable development of the human, nature, the environment, promoting healthy and aware lifestyles; to promote the Marches as a Land of well-being and quality of life at 360 degrees. In this path the Region, through the Department of Economic Development and Agriculture led by Andrea Maria Antonini, supports and contributes to A sail for all with a contribution of 10 thousand euros, within a broader call of 50 thousand euros aimed at various initiatives, but with innovative methods: prepared a crowdfunding platform through which, who promotes projects, will have to collect 10 thousand euros on the web to obtain the same amount from the Marche Region. This will amount to 20 thousand euros the funds available to "A Sailing for All", a project now active for a decade, which has been presented today in the Agriculture Hall of the regional headquarters of Palazzo Leopardi together with the institutions and the private ones who collaborate and support it: from the Municipality of Ancona, present the vice mayor Giovanni Zinni and the associate to the social services Manuela Caucci, to the territorial health company of Ancona until the entrepreneur of Frittelli Maritime Group and sailor, Alberto Rossi, supporter from the first hour of the initiative. Thanks to the contribution of many supporters who ensure the reaching of 10 thousand euros in crowdfunding, "A sail for all" gives the possibility to 24 people to attend these lessons of sailing-psychotherapy; the minimum age from 16 years, with priority for young people: the first meeting took place last Tuesday and from next Tuesday the lessons will begin every week: first theoretical then at sea and with psychotherapy. The route will last until September when participants, integrated into other crews, will dispute the traditional Conero Regatta. By virtue of the support of the Region, compared to last year, will double the number of participants who will have the possibility of crossing in Croatia: event that will be held in two weeks, between June and July, each with a crew of eight people. On board there is who stands at the helm, who to the bow, who to the frog and who maybe takes pictures or puts the music that What are these roles? There are those who are at the helm, there are those who are at the bow, who are at the rank but also those who have the role of photographer and who has the role of putting music and socializing. "Everyone has its characteristics - he adds - because, for the crew everyone also pulls out his personality and everyone is au pair, puts his resources." "They heard young people who lost the motivation to live, - remembers Grelloni -, who lost the vital drive, feel a little lost, without reason to exist. Here they experience and feel useful for someone because in a crew, we are all useful and discover that maybe it is not all there that you can live, there is still hope, there is still a possibility to be of help to yourself but also to others, to relate, to see the light, to find hope on people".
en_subtitle: From the Region Marche Land of Wellness support to the project

title: Il re del flamenco Sergio Bernal, danzo per emozionare la gente
subtitle: Tour Italia dal 28 febbraio. 'Sogno Arena di Verona e Almodovar'
content: 'La prima volta che sono salito sul palcoscenico ho capito che la danza era il mio modo di comunicare. Danzando abbiamo il potere di far emozionare la gente, che vive un momento che non dimenticherà mai. In quel momento sei sul palco e stai dando tutto te stesso per il pubblico''. Si racconta così Sergio Bernal, primo ballerino del balletto nazionale di Spagna e da molti definito il 're del flamenco', a Firenze ospite speciale della kermesse DanzainFiera. Stella del Gala conclusivo, in programma domenica pomeriggio, Bernal ha anche parlato della sua carriera e del suo tour in Italia, che partirà il 28 febbraio. Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA
en_title: King of flamenco Sergio Bernal, danzo to excite people
en_content: 'The first time I climbed on the stage I realized that dance was my way of communicating. By dancing we have the power to make people excite, who lives a moment that will never forget. At that moment you're on stage and you're giving yourself everything to the audience. This is how Sergio Bernal, the first dancer of the national ballet of Spain and from many defined the 're del flamenco', in Florence special guest of the kermesse DanzainFiera. Stella del Gala concluding, scheduled on Sunday afternoon, Bernal also spoke about his career and tour in Italy, which will leave on February 28. Reproduction reserved © Copyright ANSA
en_subtitle: Italy Tour from 28 February. 'Sogno Arena di Verona e Almodovar'

title: Captain America senza rivali nelle sale Usa, ma incassi a -70%
subtitle: Seguono The Monkey e Paddington in Perù; botteghino magro
content: Captain America: Brave new world perde slancio, ma non il primo posto tra i film più visti nelle sale di Usa e Canada. Dopo un solido esordio a 100 milioni di dollari lo scorso fine settimana con ponte festivo, l'ultimo arrivato in casa Disney e Marvel perde il 68% nel secondo weekend, con un incasso di 28,2 milioni di dollari. Nessuno nella Casa di Topolino si aspettava dal supereroe a stelle e strisce interpretato da Anthony Mackie (che sostituisce Chris Evans) l'ottima tenuta di Deadpool & Wolverine. I cinecomic sfornati dai due studi hanno abituato a tonfi anche più clamorosi. Tutto sommato, quindi, un incasso globale che sfiora i 300 milioni di dollari non è male per un film che ne è costati 180. Riproduzione riservata © Copyright ANSA
en_title: Captain America unrivaled in US theatres, but earned at -70%
en_content: Captain America: Brave new world loses momentum, but not the first place among the most watched movies in the theatres of USA and Canada. After a solid debut at $100 million last weekend with a festive bridge, the last arrived at the Disney house and Marvel lost 68% on the second weekend, with a cashout of 28.2 million dollars. No one in the House of Mickey Mouse expected from the star superhero and strips portrayed by Anthony Mackie (who replaces Chris Evans) the excellent estate of Deadpool & Wolverine.
